lines.english: What does 'a cold welcome' refer to?
lines.raw: What does 'a cold welcome' refer to? | “冷淡的欢迎”指的是什么？

lines.english: On Wednesday evening, we went to the Town Hall.
lines.chinese: 星期三晚上，我们去了市政厅。
lines.raw: On Wednesday evening, we went to the Town Hall. | 星期三晚上，我们去了市政厅。

lines.english: It was the last day of the year
lines.raw: It was the last day of the year | 那是今年的最后一天，

lines.english: and a large crowd of people had gathered under the Town Hall clock.
lines.chinese: 一大群人聚集在市政厅大钟下面。
lines.raw: and a large crowd of people had gathered under the Town Hall clock. | 一大群人聚集在市政厅大钟下面。

lines.english: It would strike twelve in twenty minutes' time.
lines.chinese: 再过二十分钟，大钟就要敲响十二点了。
lines.raw: It would strike twelve in twenty minutes' time. | 再过二十分钟，大钟就要敲响十二点了。
Fifteen minutes passed and then, | 十五分钟过去了，接着，

lines.english: at five to twelve, the clock stopped.
lines.chinese: 在十二点差五分的时候，大钟停了。
lines.raw: at five to twelve, the clock stopped. | 在十二点差五分的时候，大钟停了。

lines.english: The big minute hand did not move.
lines.raw: The big minute hand did not move. | 巨大的分针一动不动。
We waited and waited, | 我们等啊等，
but nothing happened. | 但什么也没有发生。
Suddenly someone shouted. | 突然有人喊道：
'It's two minutes past twelve! | “已经十二点过两分了！
The clock has stopped!' | 大钟停了！”
I looked at my watch. | 我看了看我的表。
It was true. | 果然如此。

lines.english: The big clock refused to welcome the New Year.
lines.chinese: 那座大钟拒不迎接新年。
lines.raw: The big clock refused to welcome the New Year. | 那座大钟拒不迎接新年。

lines.english: At that moment, everybody began to laugh and sing.
lines.chinese: 就在那一刻，所有人都开始笑啊唱啊。
lines.raw: At that moment, everybody began to laugh and sing. | 就在那一刻，所有人都开始笑啊唱啊。
